# Core Health Report

**Generated by Phase 1 & 2**

## Directory Consistency
- `core/` has been restructured to strictly adhere to the target architecture.
- All core framework files are categorized into subdirectories:
  - `core/engine/` (orchestration, execution management, correlation)
  - `core/plugin_manager/` (loader and interface)
  - `core/workflow_engine/` (workflow engine)
  - `core/config/` (configuration manager)
  - `core/logging/` (logging framework)
  - `core/dependency_manager/` (doctor)

## Package Boundaries & Import Structure
- Imports have been updated across the codebase (`reconx.py`, `tests/`, `api/`, `modules/`) to use the new nested package structure (e.g., `core.engine.orchestrator` instead of `core.orchestrator`).
- Clean separation between execution (engine), plugin discovery (plugin_manager), and workflow definitions (workflow_engine).

## Broken Imports & Circular Dependencies
- All core imports successfully pass validation without circular dependencies.
- A previous broken import in `api.gateway.main` (importing `Asset` from `core.database` instead of `core.models`) has been resolved.

## Duplicate Functionality & Unused Code
- No unused files in the `core/` namespace.
- Further consolidation of logging and error handling will proceed in Phase 5 and Phase 7.
